candidates.text: the localhost link is the most relatable part. everyones first claude code project lives on localhost for way too long because deploying feels like a whole separate skill. once you get past that gap between it works on my machine and its actually live... the whole game changes. next step: ask claude code to deploy it to netlify or vercel for you. one prompt and its done

Claude + Antigravity - Coding
Supabase/Firebase - Database + Bsckend
Vercel - Deploy
Godaddy/Name Cheap - Domain
Stripe - Payment 
Github - Version Control
Clerk - Auth
Cloud flare - DNS
Better Stack - Error Tracker
Don't over complicate it

Save this for future you will need it 🫡

you’re telling me you need an isolated vps to let claude code run in bypass permissions mode…

bro i literally run this shit on my main macbook unsupervised while im in the gym from my phone

i create a github issue and DM the url to my agent and just say “ok bro do this and open a pr once you finished. i’ll need to a set now. don’t make mistake”

this is literally the best way of working ever - why you so scared?
